openmeter/ledger/account/adapter.purpose: Ent-backed persistence layer for ledger accounts, sub-accounts, and their routing rows. Implements the ledgeraccount.Repo interface; all reads/writes must stay transaction-aware via entutils so they rebind to a tx already carried in ctx.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapping
patterns.description: Every public repo method body is wrapped in entutils.TransactingRepo so it rebinds to the ctx-carried tx instead of r.db directly.
patterns.example: func (r *repo) CreateAccount(ctx, input) (...) { return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, r, func(ctx, tx *repo) (...) { entity, err := tx.db.LedgerAccount.Create()... }) }

patterns.name: TxUser implementation
patterns.description: repo satisfies entutils.TxUser[*repo] via Tx/WithTx/Self; WithTx rebuilds db from raw tx config with entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig.
patterns.example: var _ entutils.TxUser[*repo] = (*repo)(nil); func (r *repo) WithTx(ctx, tx) *repo { return &repo{db: entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()).Client()} }

patterns.name: Map* projector functions
patterns.description: DB entities are converted to domain Data structs via exported MapAccountData / MapSubAccountData; never return raw db.* entities.

patterns.name: Idempotent ensure via OnConflict ResolveWithIgnore
patterns.description: EnsureSubAccount and resolveOrCreateRoute upsert by unique constraint columns then re-query, so duplicate routes/sub-accounts resolve to the existing row.

patterns.name: Route normalization before persistence/query
patterns.description: Routes are normalized (input.Route.Normalize()) and keyed via ledger.BuildRoutingKey before insert and in list-filter predicate building, so cost-basis canonicalization (0.70 == 0.7) is enforced.

patterns.name: Eager-load required edges then validate
patterns.description: Sub-account queries use WithRoute().WithAccount(); MapSubAccountData errors if either edge is nil.
patterns.example: if entity.Edges.Account == nil { return ..., fmt.Errorf("account edge is required") }

key_file_guides.role: repo struct, NewRepo constructor, and the TxUser plumbing (Tx/WithTx/Self) plus Repo interface assertion.
key_file_guides.watch_for: WithTx must rebuild db from the raw tx config; do not return r unchanged or transactions leak across the original client.

key_file_guides.role: Account CRUD: CreateAccount, GetAccountByID, ListAccounts, and MapAccountData projector.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Wrap all bodies in TransactingRepo; ListAccounts only filters AccountTypeIn when input.AccountTypes is non-empty.

key_file_guides.role: Sub-account ensure/get/list, the private resolveOrCreateRoute upsert, and MapSubAccountData.
key_file_guides.watch_for: resolveOrCreateRoute is the only place routes are created; route filtering in ListSubAccounts uses mo.Option presence semantics (IsPresent + nil-inner => *IsNil predicate).

key_file_guides.role: Integration tests against a real Postgres (testutils.InitPostgresDB + migrate.OMMigrationsConfig); exercises route uniqueness and cost-basis canonicalization.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Requires Postgres; tests assert entdb.IsConstraintError on duplicate routing keys and that canonicalized cost basis collides.

anti_patterns: Accessing r.db directly inside a method body instead of the tx-bound client from TransactingRepo.
Returning raw db.LedgerAccount / db.LedgerSubAccount entities instead of mapped Data structs.
Building route rows without Normalize() + BuildRoutingKey, breaking cost-basis canonical uniqueness.
Mapping a sub-account without WithRoute()/WithAccount() loaded, hitting the nil-edge error.
Implementing ensure as plain Create instead of OnConflict+ResolveWithIgnore, losing idempotency.

decisions.decision: Routes are upserted independently of sub-accounts rather than being a dependent edge.
decisions.rationale: Per subaccount.go comment: routes are shared/hidden internal rows; a standalone routes table reveals which currencies are held without sub-account grouping detail.

decisions.decision: Cost basis is canonicalized through the routing key, not stored verbatim for uniqueness.
decisions.rationale: Ensures 0.70 and 0.7 map to one sub-account/route, enforced both in BuildRoutingKey and the unique constraint (tested in TestRepo_SubAccountRouteUniquenessConstraints).

code_examples.scenario: Tx-aware ensure with upsert and re-query

openmeter/ledger/account/service.purpose: Service layer implementing ledger.account.Service over a ledgeraccount.Repo: validates inputs, maps Data structs into rich domain Account/SubAccount values, and provides advisory locking for posting. Single file service.go.

patterns.name: Validate-then-delegate
patterns.description: Mutating methods call input.Validate() first and return early on error before touching the repo.
patterns.example: func (s *service) CreateAccount(ctx, input) { if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; accData, err := s.repo.CreateAccount(ctx, input)... }

patterns.name: Data-to-domain mapping
patterns.description: Repo returns *Data; service wraps each into a domain value via account.NewAccountFromData(data, s.live) or account.NewSubAccountFromData(data).

patterns.name: Self-wired live services
patterns.description: New() sets svc.live = account.AccountLiveServices{SubAccountService: svc} so accounts can resolve sub-account posting addresses through the same service.

patterns.name: transaction.Run for multi-step writes
patterns.description: EnsureSubAccount wraps repo call in transaction.Run(ctx, s.repo, ...) so the create-and-map sequence shares one tx.

patterns.name: Deterministic lock ordering
patterns.description: LockAccountsForPosting dedupes by ID, sorts by (namespace, id), then LockForTX each, to avoid deadlocks; only customer FBO/Receivable/Accrued account types are locked.
patterns.example: sort.Slice(ids, ...); key, _ := lockr.NewKey("namespace", id.Namespace, "account", id.ID); s.locker.LockForTX(ctx, key)

patterns.name: Nil-locker tolerance
patterns.description: LockAccountsForPosting returns nil immediately when s.locker is nil, so locking is optional dependency.

key_file_guides.role: Whole service: struct {repo, locker, live}, New constructor, account/sub-account CRUD, and LockAccountsForPosting.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always pass s.live into NewAccountFromData; locking only covers customer FBO/Receivable/Accrued types and must keep the sorted-by-namespace-then-id order to stay deadlock-free.

anti_patterns: Skipping input.Validate() before delegating mutations to the repo.
Returning repo *Data structs directly instead of NewAccountFromData / NewSubAccountFromData domain values.
Forgetting to pass s.live to NewAccountFromData, breaking sub-account address resolution.
Locking accounts in arbitrary order, reintroducing deadlock risk.
Using slog.Default() or context.Background() instead of injected dependencies / propagated ctx.

decisions.decision: SubAccountService is self-wired into AccountLiveServices inside New rather than injected.
decisions.rationale: Account-specific route helpers need a concrete sub-account service to create posting addresses; the service is its own provider so no external wiring is required.

decisions.decision: Posting locks are scoped to customer account types and ordered deterministically.
decisions.rationale: Only customer FBO/Receivable/Accrued accounts participate in concurrent posting; sorted lock acquisition prevents deadlocks across concurrent transactions.

code_examples.scenario: Constructor self-wiring live services and validate-then-delegate create
